title: 1981-04-16_Pat_144.txt
key_claims: The Holy Spirit acts as an external catalyst (an "alchemical wedding") that extracts the divine/Dionysian nature (Christ) from the titanic/human nature within the individual.

Christ may not be an historical person but rather a ubiquitous end-state of the evolving ecosphere, mirroring Teilhard de Chardin's Point Omega. One does not "find" Christ; the universe evolves *into* Christ.
God's will (*Pronoia*) is the only force powerful enough to overcome the vast, deterministic power of astral determinism (*Heimarmene*) and psychological trauma.
The ultimate resolution of Christian and Stoic thought is the realization of *Felix Culpa*: the understanding that the good generated by God's will far outweighs the suffering imposed by necessity.
recurring_concepts: **Heimarmene vs Pronoia**: The conflict between the deterministic fate dictated by the past (trauma/necessity) and the liberating intervention of God's grace.
reading_excerpt: The Holy Spirit acts as an external catalyst (an "alchemical wedding") that extracts the divine/Dionysian nature (Christ) from the titanic/human nature within the individual.
